data.title: A16z doubles down on LayerZero with $55M investment
data.description:
Venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz, or a16z, announced a $55 million investment in LayerZero, a Web3 company that runs a crosschain messaging protocol. The investment was disclosed in an April 17 X post by Ali Yahya, a general partner in the firm. A16z has made previous investments in LayerZero, including an initial investment in March 2022 and a subsequent investment during LayerZero’s Series B funding round in April 2023. The companies have not disclosed the funding round's valuation
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/a16z-doubles-down-on-layer-zero-commitment-55m-investment?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Kraken has laid off hundreds of employees across its business units in the past few months.
data.description: Crypto exchange Kraken has laid off hundreds of employees across its business units over the past few months, according to people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Trump: Powell will cut interest rates sooner or later. The only thing he is good at is cutting interest rates
data.description: US President Trump: Interest rates should be lowered now. I think Fed Chairman Powell will cut interest rates at some point. Powell will face a lot of political pressure. Interest rates are pretty stable now, but they should be better.

data.title: Trump privately discussed firing Powell and replacing him with former Fed governor Warsh
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the Wall Street Journal, President Trump has been privately discussing the dismissal of Federal Reserve Chairman Powell for several months, but he has not made a final decision on whether to oust him before the end of his term next year, according to people familiar with the matter. People familiar with the matter said that at a meeting held at Mar-a-Lago, Trump discussed with former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh the possibility of firing Powell before the end of his term and possibly choosing Warsh to succeed him, but Warsh advised against firing Federal Reserve Chairman Powell and believed that he should be allowed to end his term without intervention. U.S. Treasury Secretary Bensont also opposed Trump's intention to remove Powell.

data.title: Binance confirms advisory role in state-backed Bitcoin reserve initiatives
data.description:
The world’s largest crypto exchange, Binance, is reportedly engaging several countries in a plan to create a strategic Bitcoin reserve. According to The Financial Times, the exchange CEO, Richard Teng, disclosed this in a recent interview. Teng reportedly said that several countries have approached Binance to advise them on creating a strategic Bitcoin Reserve and […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/binance-confirms-advisory-role-in-state-backed-bitcoin-reserve-initiatives/

data.title: Bank of Japan Governor: US tariff policy will hit Japan's economy
data.description: Kazuo Ueda, governor of the Bank of Japan, said on the 17th that the US tariff policy will impact the Japanese economy and may have a negative impact on the international financial capital market. Kazuo Ueda said that in addition to directly affecting trade activities, the US tariff policy has also led to increased uncertainty and impacted the confidence of businesses and households. He believes that the US tariff policy will put downward pressure on the Japanese economy. He said that it is now necessary to judge the impact of tariffs on the economic and price prospects without preconceptions, and to prudently decide on appropriate financial policies based on this.

data.title: Trump: I don't regret nominating Powell
data.description: President Donald Trump also declined to say when asked by reporters that he regretted nominating Powell for the position. "I think he's terrible, but I can't complain," Trump said, noting the strong economy during his first term. Trump then went on to complain about Powell, saying he thought the Fed chairman was "playing politics" and that Powell was "someone I never really liked."

data.title: Trump: If I ask, Fed Chairman Powell will have to resign
data.description: U.S. President Trump said: "If I ask him to, Federal Reserve Chairman Powell will have to leave. I am not satisfied with him (Federal Reserve Chairman Powell). Powell is slow and slow."

data.title:
